_monorepoNote: pnpm + Turborepo workspace: the site lives under apps/web/. App paths in any descriptive note below (src/..., lib/..., public/..., config/..., scripts/contentful/..., .env.local) resolve under apps/web/ (e.g. apps/web/src/utils/predica/sermonEntry.ts, apps/web/.env.local). Only .claude/, docs/, and tasks/ stay at the repo root. The script/output/dir paths actually executed by the harness (commands.*, predica.*.script, predica.*.logo, predica.voices.dir, etc.) are already correct as written; this note only governs the prose references.

tracker.ticketKeyNote: ICR-N is the NATIVE Jira issue key — N is the Jira issue number (not a Trello idShort). Fetch directly via getJiraIssue(cloudId, issueIdOrKey='ICR-N'); there is no board/list scan. Branches/PRs/commits and all Atlassian WRITE calls use the ICR-N key string. cloudId is required on every mcp__atlassian-divinelab__* call.
tracker.componentNote: ICR is ONE Jira project for the whole monorepo; the source app is the Jira Component (Website vs Ministry Admin Panel), not a separate project. Set components:[{name}] at create. Default a new issue's component from its work area: apps/web -> Website, apps/admin -> Ministry Admin Panel.
tracker.transitionResolutionNote: Jira issues move between STATUSES by workflow transition, not by id or list. Resolve at runtime: getTransitionsForJiraIssue(cloudId, key) -> pick the transition whose to.name === the target status name (from statuses above; compare case-insensitive/trimmed) -> transitionJiraIssue(cloudId, key, transitionId). NEVER hardcode numeric transition ids. If the target status isn't directly reachable in one hop, advance along workflow[].order without overshooting; if still unreachable, surface the drift — never invent an id.

workflow.name: In Review
workflow.setBy: /work step 14 via pr-author (In Progress -> In Review, at PR-ready)

workflow.name: In Testing
workflow.setBy: /merge (In Review -> In Testing, after squash-merge succeeds)

workflow.name: Done
workflow.setBy: HUMAN ONLY — set when the human deploys to prod and closes the issue. NEVER set by any agent or command.

automatedTransitions.gate: after PR flipped to ready + PR-link comment posted

forbiddenTransitions.reason: Human-only. /work and all agents MUST NOT transition any issue to Done. Done == merged, deployed, and closed by a human.

Task.note: default chore; use refactor/perf/feat when the work is purely that. A 'perf'/'refactor' label overrides.

tracker.issueTypeNote: Commit type derives from the Jira ISSUE TYPE (not a label): Bug->fix, Story->feat, Task->chore. Epic (id 10000) is the parent/initiative level; child issues set parent:{key:'ICR-<epic>'} at create (the system 'parent' field — Epic Link is deprecated in company-managed projects).
tracker.needsRefinementNote: Refinement state is a Jira LABEL. An intake issue (To Do) carries the 'needs-refinement' label; refine removes it via editJiraIssue. A To Do issue WITHOUT the 'needs-refinement' label is the /work-ready signal. /work reads issue labels at its entry gate (step 1.5) and stops with a guardrail if 'needs-refinement' is still present.
tracker.humanGateNote: Every tracker WRITE (transitionJiraIssue, addCommentToJiraIssue, editJiraIssue) happens at or after a human gate. /work owns exactly two automated transitions (todo->inProgress, inProgress->inReview) and posts the PR-link comment at PR-ready. It NEVER transitions an issue to Done.
tracker.migrationNote: Issues imported from the legacy Trello boards carry a 'migrated-from-trello' label plus a 'trello-web-<n>'/'trello-admin-<n>' label and a description-footer backlink to the archived Trello card. The legacy Trello idShort is NOT the Jira key (numbers differ).

qa._note: Drives /qa + qa-acceptance + the embedded /work and /merge QA steps. Reads the tracker block for the Jira project key + status names. QA targets are env-by-name (qa.env.<name>): 'preview' = the per-PR Vercel preview deployment (pre-merge QA), 'staging' = the dedicated staging env at staging.idcredentor.org / website-staging DB (post-merge QA). Production is hard-denied in EVERY env. autoMerge disabled (kill switch); merge is user-triggered via /merge.

depth.sourceNote: Jira has no default 'QA Depth' field. Resolve in priority order: (1) a 'qa-<depth>' label on the issue (e.g. qa-heavy); (2) a 'QA: <depth>' token in the issue description; (3) fall back to qa.depth.default. Reject values not in qa.depth.allowed.

preview.requirePreviewEnvironmentNote: The hostname allowlist is NECESSARY BUT NOT SUFFICIENT: the production deployment ALSO has a *.vercel.app alias (e.g. idc-redentor-website.vercel.app), which the regex would otherwise accept. Before ANY browser/API/seed action the resolver MUST confirm the resolved deployment is a PREVIEW, not Production — check mcp__claude_ai_Vercel__get_deployment.target !== 'production' (or the GitHub deployment environment === 'Preview'). Reject any deployment whose target/environment is Production even when its host ends in .vercel.app. Prefer the per-PR branch preview whose host contains '-git-<branch>-' or a unique deployment hash.
preview.baseUrlHostAllowNote: Default-deny allowlist for the QA base URL host. /qa runs ONLY against a Vercel PREVIEW deployment for the PR. Three gates, all required: (1) host matches baseUrlHostAllow (*.vercel.app); (2) host is NOT in productionHostDeny (custom domains AND the production *.vercel.app aliases); (3) the deployment's target/environment is Preview (requirePreviewEnvironment). Preview URL is discovered per-PR via mcp__claude_ai_Vercel__list_deployments / get_deployment (target=preview), or the GitHub deployment status on the PR.
preview.dbNote: Mongo backs ONLY the blog 'likes' + saved 'contact' messages, in a DB literally named 'website'. The allowlist excludes 'website' so QA never reads/writes production data. Phase 1: no Mongo writes at all.

staging.requirePreviewEnvironmentNote: Staging is NOT a PER-PR Vercel preview deployment — it has its OWN host allowlist (^staging\.idcredentor\.org$), not a *.vercel.app host. It MUST NOT inherit the preview's 'must be a Vercel preview deployment (target!==production)' check. But it MUST still hard-deny every productionHostDeny host. Net: the only sanctioned non-per-PR-preview QA target is exactly staging.idcredentor.org. CAUTION — this is a statement about the QA HOST GATE, not about the build env: staging IS a Vercel BRANCH deployment, so it is built with VERCEL_ENV=preview, which makes previewLike true there (draft content + Contentful-framable CSP, X-Frame-Options omitted). Do not read 'staging is not a preview' as 'VERCEL_ENV !== preview' — verified live: staging.idcredentor.org returns frame-ancestors including app.contentful.com and no X-Frame-Options. This is what makes staging the standing Contentful Live Preview target (docs/architecture/contentful-data-layer.md § Live Preview).
staging.liveIntegrationNote: Staging has its OWN 'website-staging' Mongo DB (created + wired in Vercel), so Mongo reads/writes against website-staging are allowed. BUT SendGrid/Resend are presumed LIVE on staging unless sandbox creds exist — so QA must NOT happy-path POST /api/subscribe or /api/contact (it would email a real recipient / hit the live audience). Form submit/validation can be exercised up to the network boundary; full end-to-end form POST is DEFERRED until staging has sandbox mail creds or a test recipient.
staging.dbNameAllowNote: Now INCLUDES 'website-staging' (the real staging DB). Prod 'website' stays excluded — never read/write it.
staging.baseUrlHostAllowNote: Default-deny allowlist for the staging QA base URL host. Two gates, both required: (1) host matches baseUrlHostAllow (^staging\.idcredentor\.org$); (2) host is NOT in productionHostDeny. requirePreviewEnvironment is false here (staging is not a Vercel preview), so the preview-only third gate does not apply — but production is still rejected by gate (2).

transitions._note: Jira status moves by transition-by-name (tracker.transitionResolution). Phase 1 report-only: /qa may transition To Do/In Progress -> In Review, never Done. When fix/auto land later, onRemediationReady moves inProgress->inReview via pr-author.

autoMerge.killSwitchNote: Phase 1 kill switch. Even when modes fix/auto are enabled later, autoMerge stays false until explicitly flipped — a human merges every PR.

reviewLoop._note: Drives the detached post-PR loop in /work after the PR is marked ready: watch code-review comments (incl. the Codex bot, which posts a few minutes after PR-ready) AND CI checks, address them, then notify the user. Driver is ScheduleWakeup (dynamic-paced, stateful, worktree-bound). If ScheduleWakeup is unavailable at runtime, /work skips the loop and falls back to a one-shot check + notify.
reviewLoop.idempotencyNote: Track addressed review-thread ids in the /work state file (addressedThreadIds). On each tick, skip threads already replied to (and cross-check the reply author == self) so a crash-and-resume never double-replies.
reviewLoop.pacingNote: First tick ~firstCheckSeconds after PR-ready (catch the Codex review + first CI signal). Idle re-checks every pollSeconds (cache-warm). After WE push a fix, wait afterPushSeconds for the fresh CI run. End on clean (all readinessRequires) OR maxIterations/totalTimeoutSeconds; notify either way.

merge._note: Drives /merge ICR-N (and the in-session 'merge' hand-off from /work). USER-TRIGGERED ONLY — qa.autoMerge.enabled stays false; nothing here is autonomous. Squash-only, refuse on red CI, clean up the worktree+branch, move In Review -> In Testing, then run post-merge staging QA. NEVER moves a card to Done.

commandsNote: typecheck maps to ICR's actual 'type-check' script (hyphen). 'test' = vitest run (single-pass; package.json 'test' is 'vitest run', never watch). 'e2e' = playwright test (config present, no specs in Phase 1). lint = eslint . If a script is absent at runtime, verifier/qa-runner reports it rather than inventing one.

contentful._note: TWO LANES (docs/architecture/contentful-environments.md). CONTENT edits happen live in production (the `master` alias env) with no workflow. MODEL changes (new/changed/deleted content type or field, or an entry remap) use a work env: DEFAULT = a permanent `staging` env (granted on the Delivery+Preview API keys ONCE), promoted to prod at cutover via Contentful Merge and/or the committed scripts/contentful/ migrations (rollback = reverse migration). HEAVY = for big BREAKING changes (type deletions, field renames, merges), build in `staging`, then a HUMAN performs the stable-name alias-swap cutover: re-point master→staging, delete the old production env, clone staging→a fresh `production`, re-point master→production (see docs/architecture/contentful-environments.md §Heavy alias-swap runbook). The single free-tier work-env slot is shared, so only one lane runs at a time. Cutover is HUMAN-ONLY, like merge/Done. Write path + safety: docs/architecture/contentful-mcp.md. Read path: docs/architecture/contentful-data-layer.md.
contentful.defaultWorkEnvNote: The standing model-change work env (permanent; set up on the API keys + MCP/.env.local/preview ONCE). For big breaking changes that need an alias-flip rollback, see the `heavyCutover` key below and docs/architecture/contentful-environments.md §Heavy alias-swap runbook — stable names (`production` + `staging`) are used throughout; semver work-env names are not.
contentful.freeTierNote: Free tier allows production (the master alias target) + exactly ONE other environment. The API enforces a quota of 1 environment beyond the alias target (creating a second fails: 'Quota reached ... 1 out of 1 allotted'). The two slots are permanently occupied by `production` (the alias target) + `staging` (the permanent work env); no per-cycle create/delete is needed. A heavy alias-swap cutover temporarily re-points the alias and swaps envs (see `heavyCutover`) but never requires a 3rd env. You can only ever delete the NON-aliased env (Contentful blocks deleting the alias target).
contentful.appEnvVarNote: Read by lib/contentful/fetch.ts. Unset/absent => the app targets the `master` alias (production) — production config NEVER sets it. Set it to the current work env (e.g. staging) in .env.local AND the branch-scoped Vercel Preview to test model changes before cutover.

heavyCutover._note: Big BREAKING model changes (type deletions, field renames, merges) use a stable-name alias-swap, NOT semver work-env names. Runbook: docs/architecture/contentful-environments.md §5.
steps: Cold-backup old production (npx contentful space export -> scripts/contentful/backups/).
Re-point master alias -> staging (live now reads the tested staging env).
Delete the old production env (frees the free-tier slot).
Clone staging -> a fresh env named `production`.
Re-point master alias -> production; refresh staging from production for the next cycle.
heavyCutover.tradeoff: Free tier holds 2 envs, so steps 1-4 have no separate rollback env; the alias serves the known-good staging the whole window, and the cold backup is the last resort.

writePath.via: Contentful MCP (@contentful/mcp-server) + committed contentful-migration scripts under scripts/contentful/
writePath.targetEnv: the current WORK env (e.g. staging) — NEVER the master alias
writePath.protectedNote: PROTECTED_ENVIRONMENTS=master,production blocks MCP WRITE tools against the live alias AND the live env. Model changes go to `staging` only; production is changed by a human cutover (Merge / committed scripts / alias-swap), never by an agent via the MCP. Reads work against any env (environmentId is a required per-call arg).

cutover.action: DEFAULT lane: apply the tested migration to production (Contentful Merge for the model and/or run the committed scripts/contentful/ migrations against the prod env). HEAVY lane: 5-step stable-name alias-swap — cold-backup old production → re-point master→staging → delete old production → clone staging→`production` → re-point master→production (see docs/architecture/contentful-environments.md §5). Either way, done at PR-merge time.
cutover.humanGateNote: HUMAN-ONLY, like merge and Done. NO agent or command ever re-points the `master` alias. Agents propose changes in the work env; a human promotes at PR-merge time.
cutover.rollback: DEFAULT lane: reverse migration or fix-forward. HEAVY lane: during the alias-swap window the alias serves the known-good, fully-tested `staging` env (never deleted); the §0 JSON cold backup (scripts/contentful/backups/) is the last-resort restore. No separate rollback env exists — that is the documented free-tier tradeoff (docs/architecture/contentful-environments.md §Heavy alias-swap runbook).

contentful.apiKeyEnvAccess: Contentful Delivery (CDA) + Preview (CPA) API keys are ENVIRONMENT-SCOPED. The app's existing tokens allow the `master` alias ONLY, so a freshly created work env returns UNKNOWN_ENVIRONMENT until it is added to BOTH keys (Contentful -> Settings -> API keys -> [key] -> Environments). Do this FIRST each cycle, before local/preview testing. After cutover the alias covers the new env automatically, so production never needs a key change. (No MCP tool manages API keys -> human action in the Contentful UI.)
oneTimeConfigTouch: Delivery (CDA) + Preview (CPA) API keys -> add `staging` (Settings -> API keys -> Environments) [human-only, once]
MCP ENVIRONMENT_ID -> staging (default; reads override per-call)
Vercel Preview CONTENTFUL_ENVIRONMENT (branch-scoped) -> staging (model-change PRs only)

playwrightProjectMap._note: Monorepo: the public site is at apps/web. ICR uses apps/web/src/app/[locale]/<segment> (NO route groups) plus apps/web/src/app/api/<route>. Phase 1 has NO e2e suite — qa-runner authors specs per-ticket on heavy depth. This map tells qa-runner which e2e project/tag to target for a given changed path.

graphify.enabledNote: auto = detect at runtime by checking ${MAIN_REPO_ROOT}/graphify-out/graph.json. false to skip; true to require.
graphify.staleWarnNote: Code freshness is handled continuously by the git post-commit hook (AST re-extract, no LLM). staleWarnSeconds targets SEMANTIC drift — doc/content/markdown changes that only a full `graphify update` re-extracts. Agents warn past this threshold and Grep to confirm negatives.
graphify.updateCommandNote: The CLI takes a SUBCOMMAND + path: `graphify update <root>`. The `graphify --update` flag form is the /graphify SKILL interface, NOT the raw CLI — passing it makes the CLI parse the path as an unknown command and silently error. Always invoke as `<updateCommand> "<root>"`.
graphify.verbsNote: Match code symbols by their node label INCLUDING parens, e.g. getPage(). query = BFS context for a question (default read path). explain "X()" = single-node onboarding: the symbol + neighbours WITH direction (`<-- caller [imports|calls]` is its dependents) — the RELIABLE impact lookup on this graph. path "A()" "B()" = shortest dependency path (data-flow traces). saveResult = persist a graph-answered Q&A to graphify-out/memory/ so repeat questions get cheaper. affected "X()" = reverse-traversal blast-radius, but only works on a DIRECTED graph; this repo's graph is currently undirected (directed:false), so prefer explain. To enable affected, rebuild once with `/graphify --directed` (graphify update preserves directedness).

policy.whoRefreshes: Continuous: the git post-commit hook (`graphify hook install`) re-extracts changed code on every commit (AST, no LLM cost). Per-session: the first /work that gets the lock additionally runs `graphify update` to catch doc/content (semantic) drift; concurrent sessions read the existing graph.
policy.whoQueries: explorer always (query/explain/path when available); implementer for caller/dep + `explain` impact lookups before edits/deletes (confirm negatives with Grep); security-reviewer for `explain` blast-radius on changed shared symbols in the diff; product-manager for reuse lookups; verifier/qa-runner/pr-author never. (`affected` is the dedicated blast-radius verb but needs a directed graph — see verbsNote.)
policy.onFailureFallback: Proceed with existing graph (or Grep/Read if no graph). Agents include staleness/error notes in their reports and Grep to confirm any 'not found' negative when the graph may be stale.
policy.memoryLoop: After answering a codebase question from the graph, the querying agent runs `graphify save-result --question ... --answer ... --nodes ...` so the next `graphify update` folds the Q&A back into the graph.

predica._note: DOMAIN BLOCK (idc-only; stays project-local per the canon domain policy). Drives /predica + the four predica-* subagents (transcriber, writer, publisher, whatsapp). Local on-demand sermon pipeline: a recording -> transcript -> bilingual sermon.json -> two branded PDFs (Card C) -> a Contentful DRAFT in production -> a ready-to-paste WhatsApp text. TWO human gates (transcript correction; promote/publish). Draft-only + send-only: the publisher's MCP allowlist omits every publish_* and writes only a DRAFT to production (never publishes); nothing is ever auto-published or auto-sent. See tasks/specs/sermon-pipeline.md §7-§9.
predica.audioInboxNote: Where Sunday recordings land. `/predica` with no path picks the NEWEST audio file here. Spaces/accents in filenames are expected; the slug is derived from the TITLE, not the filename (§7.1).
predica.artifactsDirNote: Per-sermon working dir tasks/predicas/<sermonDate>_<slug>/ holds the WAV (throwaway), transcript.{txt,srt,json}, audio.mp3, the original .m4a archive copy, sermon.json, both PDFs, contentful-entry artifacts, and whatsapp.txt. The dir is date-prefixed (YYYY-MM-DD_slug) for chronological self-sorting; the public slug/URL stays bare — only the folder carries the date. gitignored (large, not source).
predica.contentfulEnvNote: The publisher creates (or, on a regenerate, updates in place) the sermon as a DRAFT in `production` (the live env behind the master alias) via the committed CMA scripts — none has a publish call, so nothing goes live until a human reviews both locales and clicks Publish at Gate 2. The scripts refuse the `master` ALIAS by name (you write the `production` ENV, never the alias). The MCP server runs PROTECTED_ENVIRONMENTS=master,production as a backstop, which does not affect these scripts (they use the management token directly, not the MCP).
predica.siteBaseUrlNote: Production canonical used to build the deterministic share URL ${siteBaseUrl}/es-AR/predicas/<slug>. NOT the local .env.local NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL (which is http://localhost:3000 in dev). The human verifies/corrects this URL at Gate 2 if the live domain differs.

whisper.prompt: Prédica en español. Iglesia de Cristo Redentor. Jesús, evangelio, Espíritu Santo, gracia, arrepentimiento, salvación, Escritura.
whisper.note: large-v3-turbo is fast (~3-5 min for a 60-min sermon on the M3 Pro). The prompt seeds church vocabulary. -sns = suppress non-speech tokens. transcribe(audioPath) is a swappable boundary so V2 can drop in a cloud provider.

audio.note: ffmpeg makes a throwaway 16k mono WAV for whisper, and a browser-universal .mp3 (96k) for the web audio player; the original .m4a is kept as the archive.

pdf.note: Card C's generator (consumed as a black box): node <script> <sermon.json> [--out <dir>]. Needs Chromium: `pnpm exec playwright install chromium` on a fresh checkout.

featured.note: Generates the 1200x630 sermon featured image (also the OG/social card): node <script> <sermon.json> [--out <dir>] [--prompt <txt>] [--no-ai] [--regenerate]. AI background via Google Gemini (REST, reads GEMINI_API_KEY from env/.env.local, name only) + branded title/date overlay rendered with Playwright. DEGRADES GRACEFULLY: no key or any API failure -> on-brand typographic fallback card, still exits 0. Same Chromium dependency as the PDF. Canonical typed helpers + tests: src/utils/predica/featuredCard.ts. The image is a DRAFT DEFAULT the human reviews/replaces at Gate 2.

predica.entryBuilderNote: Deterministic builder: turns sermon.json (+ resolved link ids) into the exact Contentful `fields` payloads (bibleVerse + sermon), incl. localized wrapping and rich-text from content blocks. Canonical typed impl + tests: src/utils/predica/sermonEntry.ts. The publisher feeds these fields to entryCreator (below).
predica.writePathNote: All Contentful WRITES go through these THREE CMA scripts (binary-upload + create/update-entry + delete), NOT the MCP write tools — reliable at any size (a ~20MB mp3, a 50KB+ rich-text entry) where MCP base64 / LLM round-trip is infeasible. create-contentful-entry.mjs creates a draft OR updates one in place (--id <entryId>, full-fields replace, used when /predica regenerates an existing sermon). delete-contentful.mjs is the ONLY delete path (unpublish-then-delete by explicit id; --guard-referenced refuses to delete a bibleVerse still linked by another sermon/the Creed). All three are DRAFT-ONLY (no publish call) and HARD-REFUSE the master environment; they read CONTENTFUL_MANAGEMENT_ACCESS_TOKEN from env/.env.local (name only, never printed). The publisher's MCP allowlist is READ-ONLY, so it structurally cannot publish or touch master by any path.

assetUploadPolicy.note: The CMA upload endpoint handles any size, so the audio mp3, both PDFs, AND the generated featuredImage are uploaded + linked automatically. featuredImage is produced by config.predica.featured (build-predica-featured.mjs) as a DRAFT DEFAULT — the human reviews/replaces it at Gate 2 before publishing. durationSeconds is set from ffprobe.

voices.note: Local-only (gitignored) per-preacher voice profiles, one <preacher-slug>.md per preacher (slug derived from the preacher full name with the same transliterate->lowercase->dash-collapse rule as the title slug). TWO ZONES: Zone A = human-curated canonical voice guide (seeded once by the coach on first creation, NEVER auto-overwritten thereafter; the coach only appends to its fenced '🤖 Refinamientos sugeridos' list, which the human promotes by hand); Zone B = machine append-only dated observation log, one concise entry per sermon keyed by sermonDate (idempotent — a transcript-reuse re-run never double-appends). SOURCE OF LEARNING = the corrected transcript.txt ONLY, never the generated sermon.json (avoids a model-style feedback loop). PRIVATE BY DESIGN: the repo is PUBLIC and all sermon-derived content already stays out of git (.gitignore: tasks/predicas/), so these profiles live in that same private tier; in-file dated history replaces git history. STYLE, not doctrine — describes HOW the preacher communicates, never WHAT they assert. See docs/architecture/predica-voice-profiles.md.

voiceCoach.blockingNote: Step 2.5 — runs between Gate 1 (corrected transcript) and the writer so each sermon's authentic voice is captured BEFORE that sermon is written and the writer benefits immediately. NON-BLOCKING + RESILIENT: on {ok:false} or any error the orchestrator prints a warning and CONTINUES (the writer falls back to today's transcript-only voice inference). Additive — it can never break publishing. Runs on transcript-reuse (self-skips via the date idempotency check) and on --dry-run (local-only, improves the writing).
voiceCoach.source: corrected transcript only (NEVER the run's generated sermon.json)

transcriptReview.note: Gate 1 — the orchestrator stops in-conversation and waits while the human corrects transcript.txt (names, scripture refs, theology). Never delegated, never auto-skipped.

promote.note: Gate 2 — the orchestrator presents the summary and stops. All media (audio, both PDFs, the generated featuredImage) is already attached to the DRAFT in production; the human reviews both locales + the featured image (replacing it with a photo if preferred), Publishes, and pastes the WhatsApp text. No agent publishes or sends.

audit.note: A Mongo `sermons` audit collection is NOT used. Re-run idempotency is handled without extra infra: (1) pre-flight matches the recording's sourceSha256 against tasks/predicas/*/ to reuse a corrected transcript (skip transcription + Gate 1); (2) Gate 0 looks the sermon up in Contentful by canonical slug (+ sermonDate) and, on human approval, UPDATES the existing entry in place (create-contentful-entry.mjs --id) instead of creating a `-2` duplicate, then cleans up superseded assets + orphaned legacy verses via delete-contentful.mjs. Bible verses dedup site-wide by scripture coordinate (book+chapter+verse-range), reused across sermons + the Creed; never the old per-sermon internalName key.
